transcript.text: Doing well.
Just another busy week, you know, slowly catching up on things.
How about you?
Good.
Working my way through a bunch of storms over here.
Oh, damn.
Yeah.
Not sure what happened.
We're not supposed to have storms this time of the year, but yeah, it was super windy.
And then I realized, so Portugal, obviously, as in the rest of Europe, you don't have dryers, so you kind of have air
So yeah, it was fun.
know, my clothes got washed like three times already.
yeah, interesting.
Yeah, that defeats the purpose of hang-drying now, doesn't it?
I didn't even think about that.
Yeah, yeah.
So it's always a problem in winter, but that's all good.
Is your, I guess your touring's finished this week, right?
Sweet.
All done.
All done.
I'm home.
I'm nice and home for all of a month.
And then got one random show in LA, then I'm taking a vacation, which is very exciting.
So yeah, cool.
Yeah, it'll be great.
Do you have any fun trips coming up?
Nothing major.
Probably something over New Year's sometime, but yeah, next one is only in January.
So I just got back and I'm like, yeah, I want to stay home for some time.
So nice.
Yeah.
I co-signed that.
How are you doing, Mara?
Good to see you.
Thank you.
Good to see you, too.
You feeling better?
Yes.
Thank you for asking.
Yeah, I'm feeling much better, which is good because I'm going out of town for until next Thursday.
So I'll miss, yes, I'm going to Argentina with my mother.
Wow.
That's quite the trip.
I know.
I was telling Vivek, I hadn't realized it's like an 11-hour flight from where I am.
I was going to say, that's a long travel for a week.
I know.
Yeah, it was one of those things where I kind of said yes and then retroactively was like, interesting.
That's going to be a bit short.
But yeah, it'll be fun.
So I'm glad I'm feeling a little bit better.
I packed a ton of vitamin C.
So, but how are you doing?
Are you on your, like, lull?
You don't have to travel as much anymore?
Good.
Very much so, which is phenomenal.
Um, very nice.
I get to, I get to establish a normal routine at home for like a whole month.
So I cannot complain about that.
I can catch up on sleep, not leave during the weekends.
I'm very excited.
Yeah.
That sounds amazing.
Yeah.
Yeah.
But it is, uh, it is nice.
And so things are, things are moving smoothly here.
I do believe Connor is joining us.
He has a bit of an announcement, but I won't steal the thunder from him.
So, um, yeah, we can get started and if you think he should join whenever he's got some free time.
Awesome.
All right.
Yeah.
there's only two of the updates.
Um, we staged four articles last week.
Um, five currently in queue.
I think Nika staged three more, um, yesterday.
I believe, but yeah, he'll send the full update when everything is done.
Eight articles currently in review, and we're on track to deliver five this week.
Five mainly because some of the, we have some competitor articles slated for this week.
We're going to push that to next week, mainly because we're just still digesting everything in the product marketing folder, as you said, Jackson.
So yeah, those will come by next week.
Then in terms of performance, shifted to a monthly reporting because it kind of makes sense.
Obviously, we have this little tip over here in September when we were having those snag issues.
But generally, based on one week's performance, I mean, we're headed in the right direction, obviously.
So hopefully this continues.
In terms of cluster performance, all clusters, almost universally, saw higher traffic.
The model hub traffic is a bit volatile, mainly because we have a low number of pages.
The
Something interesting, we are seeing, like, according to SEMrush, we're ranking in the number one spot for a lot of model versus sort of keywords.
Like, we have an article on DeepSeek versus O1.
We have, I think, two more pages that are in number one.
But it's not the model hub pages, it's the old blog pages.
So, obviously, once the number of pages in this hub increases, we, you know, there's good signs that it should attract a lot more traffic.
So, yeah.
And in terms of the comparison to Arise, it's still kind of holding position.
They seem to be losing about three keywords a week.
We're losing the same amount.
So, yeah, it's kind of going the same way.
In terms of LLM referral traffic, this past week, we saw an increase.
And, again, know, this is generally in the right direction.
GPT is...
Still the top referrer.
And scrumstata, again, not much change from last week.
The prompt category rankings are pretty much the same for all the prompts that we were tracking.
So that's the performance update.
Any questions I can answer?
No, this all makes sense to me.

I think what I'm thinking about right now is just kind of next steps as far as like more keywords or phrasing within the observability bucket and just trying to see if there's anything that we haven't covered yet.

And then hausing a bit on competitor besides the refreshes of the ones with the new knowledge base, I think we've pretty much covered a lot of the top ones that we would want to go after in the immediate sense.
So reshifting some of that to either more model hub pages or more net new blogs in the content buckets that we aren't performing as well in is kind of what I'm
I'm thinking about next, if that makes sense for you guys' team.
Yeah, I think the redirecting into Model Hub makes sense.
We can split that across the new categories that Conor suggested, which was AI agent engineering slash the building of the AI agent engineer kind of content cluster.
eval engineering.
The eval engineering, sorry, yeah.
Yeah, that'll be next.
Yeah, so generally, we're good in terms of upcoming sort of topics, et cetera.
Should flesh those out pretty quickly.
And then, yeah, the only discussion point, I think, this was one you had raised in Airtable regarding the organization of the pillar pages.
So we had one on LLM evaluation, and then we had one on AI agent evaluation as well.
I think there were some questions around, you know, do we need so many pages on top over there?
And then the second discussion point.
The point was the template over here for the competitor page.
Yeah.
I'm happy to tackle either one, depending on...
Before we get in there, I have two comments on the layout of the Model Hub pages and the Pillar pages that I think might be worth considering.
I just want to make sure we get those in while we're on the topic, and then we can pivot to more on the Pillar pages and the competitor bar.
I hope that works for everyone.
Sure.
So I really like what you've done here with the Model Hub pages.
Hopefully everyone can see my screen or starting to see my screen.
I think this is a nice way to lay this out, and it works well, Jackson, with how you are highlighting these sections.
I know Jackson's already thinking through what the next layer of the resource here.
Um...

My recommendation, Jackson, and I think this relates here, is that basically we take the, take this page here, and I think it's great to have this kind of overview, but also to include how they're highlighted here, so that there is kind of an overview page, both for Pillar Pages, and for Model Hub pages, and for the eBooks.

In the case of the Model Hub pages, and the Pillar Pages, I'd suggest including some sort of like CTA banner at the bottom, that's relevant, maybe it's about the leaderboard, if we, if we don't want it to be like a down funnel, maybe it's about a master multi-agent systems, but as this builds out, you can kind of move it into the middle, even let's say if there's 15 models, you can have six models, models to the bottom, but I think this format, and the ability to have that there, will not only help you organize the resources page, but also provide
We had some extra places to call folks to action from there.
And specifically, I wonder if it's worth doing out that there's, what, five pillar pages live here currently and more to come.
Setting up the learn section as like a subsection here.
I don't know that you want to call it pillar pages, but you can call it learning pillars, for example.
And it could be this same format to make life easy.
And it could just be like each of the pillar pages has a link here and people can go deeper in it.
But then, again, to have like a CTA banner here.
Because I think that will help you organize what's happening under the sub banner here.
And it'll connect to these different topics together.
Because right now, it's just like, okay, different links.
Then it'll feel like a cohesive learning pathway.
And I think that'll set up well for the addition of the course, which is coming in December.
And other survey data as well to have those subsections.
Sweet.
So just to clarify, are you saying that we should have it?
Index for all of the pillar pages, and then, okay, so an index for all the pillar pages, then like mid-page CTAs for the indexes for Model Hub.
That would be suggestion.
I mean, like, your call, but I think it gives you an extra conversion opportunity, and it also lets people kind of see them holistically together.
And since we're leaning into building a course already, I think you can then feature that as the CTA on the learn pages and kind of just have those float back and forth.
Yeah, I agree with that.
I agree with that.
I think as far as the nav organization, that's kind of a question mark to me.

I'll think about that, because if we want to have, like, almost a sub-navigation where it's, like, pillars or something like that, then I feel like we may want to break this apart even further into, like, four-point situation.-navigation, because it's
It might make sense to even have the Mastering of the AI series and all these e-books start to have their own similar page.
Now, I don't know if you want to do that, maybe you combine that with the pillar page.
Yeah, mean, yeah, something to think of books and then the course, and that's kind of like the learning segment.
Yeah, how we index this is going to be a question mark moving forward because a resource hub would be ideal versus like just a ridiculous navigation.
But with framers configuration and everything there, it's kind of a nightmare.
I'll think about that from a logistics standpoint.
That was, and I also think a competitor comparison hub might make sense too, depending on how many we go.
Like not, probably not, I mean, what we have, what, five or six done so far, but there's an additional 15 you could add, right?
So, well, we have, yeah, we have them, we have a decent amount in.
In blog form, the GrowthX team is redrafting them based on all the new assets we just gave.
And then I think figure out just the navigation side of it is going to be the tricky part.
I also think that hubs will make sense so we can have more assets and then only really highlighting the most important ones in the navigation as like sub navigations is a good strategy.

By the time we hit Q1 next year, I mean, even if we pause competitor pages for a few weeks here, but if you start doing them again in December and just do one a month, one a week, pretty rapidly, you're going to have 20 competitor pages.
And there's a variety, there's enough folks that you're going to want to steal traffic from at a certain point.
So by March, you'd want to have some sort of hub or in-spoke model for that because otherwise it's going to be hard to hide the information.
Yeah.
Yep.
Okay.
So I would, I would just plan for it now because I think it'll make your life easier in the long run.
For sure.
For the competitor hub, mean, we can follow the model of Gusto that I highlighted.
mean, I think it's pretty well organized and with the dropdowns and elements.
So that would certainly, could also pick up like in terms of SEO, like alternatives and comparisons, et cetera.
I think so, yeah.
So it's a pretty good play.
But I think that would make a lot of sense as the next step.
Yeah.
Yeah.
I think that, I think that makes sense for sure.
I'd have to work through.
Oh yeah.

So there, the page that you sent for Gusto is really solid and that's like almost a good take on a way to do an index for a page or for like a competitor hub, just because it does have all the like actual guides at the bottom.
And then it like almost is like a compelling index that leads you to all of the more in-depth assets.
Yeah.
I like a little dropdown as well with the summary table, you know, that gives you all the information you need quickly.
And then, yeah, I think it's a little too long.
You know, that case study over there is a little too long, I think, on page, but yeah.
Like the individual ones by competitor?
No, let me just reshare.
So, this portion over here, Snapchat is 15 hours a month, like, it's pretty massive, mean, it's an entire, like, two scrolls to get past it.
Yeah, that's just bad UX.
That's just, that's just bad, bad design.
That's fine, we can avoid that.
This part is pretty nice.
I mean, this is just a, like, we have a why Galileo wins kind of a section in our competitor blogs already, so this is just a copy-paste of that, and then we just put it over here.
But yeah, the guides themselves are pretty good.
So, yeah, I think it's a good model to follow with some, you know, with some tweaks.
Yeah.
Yeah, I will, I'll work with our web dev agency to see what's possible here, just because some of this may be a bit difficult on the framer side of things, but it shouldn't be that hard.
It may just take a little bit longer than I would hope.
All right.
The wireframe as well looked pretty good to me.
So, yeah, I think the copy length is about right, generally.
So, yeah, because it's a landing page and I think it hits everything.
So I didn't have anything to add over here.
So, yeah.
Sweet.
That works for me.
And then as long as we feel like this is something we can templatize and reuse for each competitor with, like, minor tweaks, you know, depending on...
Our sticking points per competitor, I think then we should be in a good spot for them to kind of take it and run with it, at least get mock-ups for us to review.
Yeah.
Sounds good.
Cool.
So I think that was pretty much it.
And then, yeah, we'll send you the updates once the pages are staged and, yeah, keep moving forward with this.
Cool.
good.
Oh, I was going to jump into my stuff, but you go first.

I was going to say, going back to what we were talking about earlier around pillar page organization as far as, like, the page-to-page organization, I think what we can do for future topics is kind of map them out, like, in a sequential order just so we don't get too expansive as far as the navigation is concerned.
Like, feel like AI evaluations and then subtopics of different types of...

You know, agent evaluations into something else does make sense versus having that all just be in one or all be individual pages just so we don't get the sprawl of, you know, and we just have it organized by subtopic.
But I'm open to feedback or interpretation there if we think there are better ways to do it.
So you mean stick the subtopic on the main pillar page or?
Or like have, yeah, so like AI evaluations, you said it was more about evaluating AI systems versus agent evaluations is more about evaluating agents.

But I think if we maybe make like the primary AI evaluation page more broad, and then it could be like evaluating AI systems is two, evaluating AI agents is three, and then just having a breakdown that way versus having them all be singular pillar pages without sequential pages at the moment, if that makes sense.
So when someone gets to like an AI.
iEvaluations page, they have more pages to go through directly there after that versus like having to just click around in the nav, guess.
Yeah, makes sense.
Yeah, from our end, the reason we did that was because it was just a pure SEO play, to be honest.
And we didn't anticipate having more than five or six of these.
So we figured it's worth like, you know, going into it.
But yeah, I think, did you have more pillar pages in mind?
Like, I'm assuming there's one about guardrails, maybe one about observability.
Yeah.
Then I don't think we have a reliability one.
No, we have a testing one.
So yeah, I think that's about it.
Yeah, I commented on your doc about the, I think you had like a guardrail hub sort of idea on there.
And I feel like that made sense as a pillar page, like, you know, AI guardrails is the primary.
And then...
More in-depth pages about each of the types of guardrails that you specified.
Okay.
Makes sense.
We can queue that up.
Okay.
Cool.
All right.
We'll do it that way.
And, yeah, when the time comes, we'll maybe move that agent devals page into the AI devals page as a subsection or something.
Cool.
Cool.
Works for me.
Awesome.
The other thing I wanted to bring up is this is going be my last meeting with you all here at Galileo.
So I am departing the company next week.
I'm taking a couple weeks off before my new gig starts in December.
But it's been a distinct pleasure working with you all.
And I would love to stay in touch and super excited to see the continued impact and what you've already done as well for the company as well.
I'm very thankful for it.
Wow.
We're going to miss hanging out.
We're going to miss hanging out.
I almost said hanging out.
I'm going to miss working with That's you.
That's you.
I appreciate it.
But really excited for you.
Congratulations.
Thank you.
Our loss, their gain.
Yeah, we'll absolutely keep in touch.
But yeah, congratulations.
Awesome.
And I'm going to toss my personal contact info, if either of you need it, into the meeting chat here.
I think Marcel has it too.
But yeah, I would love to stay in touch.
I think you both do fantastic work.
And the GrowthX team overall is just doing something really special in this space.
Thank you.
I appreciate that.
Yeah, thank you for sharing this.
Excited to see where you land.
We'll keep an eye out on the LinkedIn to see where it is.
Yeah, it should be fun.
I'll share that first week of December.
Cool.
All right.
Well, enjoy your time off too.
Thank you.
forward to it.
Yeah.
Cool.
Oh, well, thank you so much.
We will miss you and we'll stay in touch.
And if anything else pops up between now and then, let us know.
Jackson, glad that we had so much overlap and like...
Any questions or anything, obviously.
I think you're in good hands, yeah.
Yeah.
Cool.
We'll keep doing the thing, even in his absence.
Yeah.
Cool.
All right.
All right.
Well, great.
Thanks, everyone.
Yeah, thank you, and good luck.
Yeah, good luck, Conor.
Yeah.
All right, talk to you all soon.
Bye.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Review content performance and define a new, hub-based site structure.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=286.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**New Site Structure:** Adopted a hub-and-spoke model for content (Pillar Pages, Model Hub, Competitors) to improve user experience and consolidate SEO value.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=550.0)
  - [**Content Strategy Shift:** Prioritizing new content for the Model Hub and "Eval Engineering" cluster, while pushing competitor articles to next week to fully digest new product marketing assets.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=309.0)
  - **Performance:** Monthly reporting is now standard. The Model Hub is volatile due to low page count, but existing blog pages are already ranking \#1 for key "model vs." keywords, validating the hub's potential.
  - [**Team Update:** Conor Bronsdon announced his departure from Galileo next week.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=1290.0)

## Topics

### Content Performance & Strategy

  - [**Production Status:**](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=286.0)
      - [Staged: 4 articles last week, 5 in queue.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=286.0)
      - [In Review: 8 articles.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=288.0)
      - [Delivery: 5 articles this week.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=288.0)
  - [**Performance Metrics:**](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=326.0)
      - [Reporting is now monthly for better trend analysis.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=326.0)
      - [Overall traffic is trending up.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=326.0)
      - [The Model Hub is volatile due to a low page count.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=346.0)
      - **Key Insight:** Existing blog pages are already ranking \#1 for "model vs." keywords (e.g., DeepSeek vs. O1), validating the Model Hub's future traffic potential.
      - [Competitor Arise is losing keywords at the same rate as Galileo.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=395.0)
      - [LLM referral traffic increased, with GPT remaining the top referrer.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=409.0)
  - [**Strategy Shift:**](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=309.0)
      - [**Priority:** Shift focus from competitor articles to new content for the Model Hub and the "Eval Engineering" cluster.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=309.0)
      - [**Rationale:** This allows the team to fully digest new product marketing assets before redrafting competitor content.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=309.0)

### New Site Structure: Hub-and-Spoke Model

  - [**Problem:** The current flat site structure is difficult to navigate and dilutes SEO value across many individual pages.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=757.0)
  - [**Solution:** Adopt a hub-and-spoke model to create cohesive user journeys and consolidate SEO authority.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=757.0)
  - [**Implementation Plan:**](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=550.0)
      - [**Pillar Pages:** Create a central index page for all pillars.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
          - [**Rationale:** Provides a cohesive learning pathway, setting up the December course launch.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
          - [**New Structure:** Consolidate subtopics (e.g., "AI Agent Evaluation") under a single parent pillar page ("AI Evaluation") to prevent navigation sprawl.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=1105.0)
      - [**Model Hub:** Create a central index page for all model comparisons.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=571.0)
          - [**Rationale:** Consolidates traffic and SEO value as the hub grows.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=571.0)
      - [**Competitor Hub:** Create a central index page for all competitor comparisons.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=821.0)
          - [**Rationale:** Essential by Q1 2026, when the number of pages is projected to reach \~20.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=864.0)
          - [**Model:** Use the Gusto competitor page as a template, adapting its structure (e.g., summary tables) while avoiding its poor UX (e.g., overly long case studies).](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=900.0)
  - [**Technical Note:** Jackson will consult the web dev agency on Framer's limitations for implementing this new structure.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=1028.0)

### Team Update: Conor Bronsdon's Departure

  - [Conor Bronsdon announced his departure from Galileo, with his last day being next week.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=1290.0)
  - [He will take a break before starting a new role in December.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=1291.0)
  - [He shared his personal contact info to stay in touch.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=1331.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=309.0)
      - [Push competitor articles to next week.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=309.0)
      - [Prioritize content for the Model Hub and "Eval Engineering" cluster.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)
      - [Queue up the "AI Guardrails" pillar page with a hub-and-spoke structure.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=1247.0)
  - [**Jackson:**](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=1028.0)
      - [Consult the web dev agency on implementing the new hub-and-spoke structure in Framer.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=1028.0)
      - [Provide mock-ups for the competitor hub based on the Gusto template.](https://fathom.video/share/sJiSgQRqc8s3Gy9z9Mu1JyzW87bvvqpA?tab=summary&timestamp=900.0)

action_items.description: Audit observability keywords; draft net-new blog topics
Draft AI agent engineering + eval engineering topics; then redirect relevant blog posts to Model Hub
Design Learn section (Pillars + eBooks + Course) w/ CTA; then build/publish index pages
Design Competitor Comparison Hub w/ dropdown + summary; then build/publish index page
Create reusable competitor comparison page template; then produce mockups for review
Draft AI guardrails pillar page + subtopics; then build/publish
